title: lithocarpus merrittii
intro: Lithocarpus merrittii, also known as Merritt's tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus merrittii include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Lithocarpus merrittii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
